programmes.show_description: Well-Schooled in Murder: Lynley and Havers respond to a call for help from a friend working at a prestigious public school to investigate the disappearance of a promising pupil.
Someone Else's Baby: More laughs with the Essex sisters. Sharon and Tracey decide to search through their mother's belongings for undiscovered treasure.
Think Gently of the Erring: Corcoran is staggered to discover someone close to him in chains. Dr Matthew Freeman approaches Robert Morehouse to suggest a joint effort.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Keeping Up Appearances: No, not Mrs Bucket - Sharon and Tracey! Tracey is shocked to find that two years in prison have finally taken their toll on Darryl.
Security: While Quinnan tries to catch the person who is threatening his friend, finding the right moment to propose becomes increasingly difficult. More grief for Polly!
New. A Pain in The....: Julia's friend makes her an enticing business offer with a cryptic suggestion, while Archie deals with a traffic warden suffering from sciatica.
New. Shine a Light On: Jimmi learns he is not cut out for public speaking when nerves get the better of him as he gives a lecture on forensics.
When Lightning Strikes Twice: Tom goes behind Zubin's back to undertake a new procedure on a patient with severe angina, and Rosie is forced to confront her true feelings for Mubbs.
Chaos Theory: Jordan rallies the team after introducing an unpopular new policy, and a boy is suspected of taking and selling LSD when he is admitted after falling from a school building.
Security: While Quinnan tries to catch the person who is threatening his friend, finding the right moment to propose becomes increasingly difficult. More grief for Polly!
New. A live episode of EastEnders in which the Walford residents enjoy Ricky and Bianca's wedding reception and the identity of Archie's killer is revealed.
New. While the Square comes to terms with recent events, an irate Zainab gets stuck in the unit and her waters break. Meanwhile, Ian and Jane discover Lucy alone with Leon.
The Healing Touch: A trip through time to more gentle days with the Yorkshire vets, one of whom went on to play a famous Time Lord. The vets visit Granville Bennett's animal hospital.
Sarah is caught trying to steal a crucial piece of evidence from Ken's office, and Emma tells Jan about Tom's true feelings.
The Sticking Place: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders threatened with eviction.
Women's Troubles: More laughs from the two cockney sparrows, Sharon and Tracey. Whilst Sharon is out enjoying herself with her new fella, Tracey is convinced she is pregnant.
The Second Stag Night of Doggy Wilkinson: The world's longest-running sitcom! Absent-minded Doggy is disappointed that his stag night isn't as exciting as his first.
What Happened to the Horse?: Popular sitcom. A innocent woodland becomes a legendary site of ghostly activity thanks to an inventive rumour spread by Alvin, Truly and Clegg.
Variations on a Theme of Road Rage: Roy Clarke's long-running sitcom about a group of sprightly seniors. When Howard buys a used car, it attracts the attention of a burly local.
New. Death by Vinyl: Final Chapter: Mattie investigates another murder involving the band when someone is stabbed with a knife, and it appears an incident from the past may be the key.
New. The Q Word: Mo McIntyre's grand plan to dig up an ancient stash of paramilitary weaponry gets under way, but he is unaware that he is already under surveillance and that MI5 are onto him.
James Bolam stars in the middle-aged detective drama. The team must identify a murder victim from just her torso, with the help of a bizarre pathologist.
The Sticking Place: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders threatened with eviction.
Payment in Blood: The detectives head to the Scottish Highlands to investigate the murder of a respected playwright, only to be confronted by glamorous stars from the film industry.
Keeping Up Appearances: No, not Mrs Bucket - Sharon and Tracey! Tracey is shocked to find that two years in prison have finally taken their toll on Darryl.
The Fine Ould Irish Gintleman: Corcoran questions the motives behind strongholds of the Five Points community, and solicits coppers of the Sixth Precinct to suss out the truth.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Tinker, Tailor: Comedy from the famed Essex siblings. Sharon begins to wonder if her husband was right when he told her there was a 'grass' planted in his cell.
Up in Smoke: Part 1: Jim takes the whisky in a tea cup a bit too far. Knowledge of his drinking is now widespread, and his colleagues are becoming increasingly frustrated.
New. The Awakening: A guilt-ridden man who was involved in a hit-and-run accident receives help from Daniel when recurring dreams begin to dominate his thoughts.
New. Tear Away: Archie steps in to resolve a dispute between a therapist and a neighbour from hell who is causing her to lose business by playing his music too loud.
In the Line of Fire: Diane visits an Army hospital to instruct its surgical team on trauma techniques, and immediately takes a shine to a captain, but is also placed in a tricky situation.
Only the Lonely: Jordan declares the Clinical Decisions Unit open for business, but the new system leaves Adam struggling to keep up. Edward tries to apologise to Ruth.
Up in Smoke: Part 1: Jim takes the whisky in a tea cup a bit too far. Knowledge of his drinking is now widespread, and his colleagues are becoming increasingly frustrated.
New. Masood heroically helps Zainab deliver their son, while Max and Jean worry about Stacey. A heartbroken Dot loses Dotty, Ronnie shuns her family, and Ian teaches Lucy a lesson.
New. While Masood shows off his new son, an unexpected visitor arrives. Lucas stops Jordan digging away at Trina's memory, Jane supports Lucy, and Jean and Becca worry about Stacey.
City Slicker: The great Christopher Timothy gets stuck into more tricky veterinary work in the show we all remember. Appearances prove deceptive when an old friend of James's visits.
A private investigator spies on Leo and Abby. Meanwhile, Ken schemes against Sir Edward, and Charles questions Sarah's loyalty.
A Serpent's Tooth: After a fatal accident in a warehouse, the youngest daughter of a carpet king hires Frank and Lu to investigate her own sisters.
Cheat!: The comedy which never runs out of Essex girl jokes. Dorien invites Tracey to a posh do. Sharon earns some cash by flogging some pirate Rolling Stones t-shirts.
In Which Howard Gets Double Booked: Young-at-heart sitcom. Howard is supposed to be taking Pearl and Marina out to dinner at the same time. How can he be in two places at once?
Will the Nearest Alien Please Come In: Popular sitcom. Sci-fi addict Kevin believes that aliens may have landed. Truly, Clegg and Alvin help to convince him that he's right.
Elegy for Small Creature and Clandestine Trackbike: More pensioner power in Yorkshire. Howard finds his new bike is hard to hide from Pearl, and Clegg only adds to his worries.
The Fire In The Sky: The padre has his work cut out when the discovery of a body in a locked room prompts local speculation to mount that Kembleford is under threat of an alien invasion.
Acclaimed drama series about a police sergeant and the man who destroyed her family. Catherine gets a tip-off about Tommy, while Helen learns that Ann has been abducted.
Lady's Pleasure: James Bolam and Dennis Waterman team up for the Beeb's hit cop caper. Pullman re-investigates the death of Nancy Murray, a housewife who died in a car crash.
A Serpent's Tooth: After a fatal accident in a warehouse, the youngest daughter of a carpet king hires Frank and Lu to investigate her own sisters.
